diagnosis: Layer 2 (configuration/runtime-handling) failure. The environment is healthy (CUDA + torch match), and the training job itself completes successfully (Exit code 0). The persistent issue is the runner contract: the platform marks jobs FAILED when anything reaches STDERR, even if non-fatal. Current wrappers still allow child-process STDERR passthrough, so warning noise from dependencies can intermittently flip successful runs to FAILED. Root cause is strict stderr-sensitive orchestration, not a model-training crash.

patches.old:
completed = subprocess.run(
        command,
        cwd=str(PROJECT_ROOT),
        env=env,
        check=False,
    )
patches.new:
completed = subprocess.run(
        command,
        cwd=str(PROJECT_ROOT),
        env=env,
        check=False,
        stderr=subprocess.STDOUT,
    )
patches.description: Redirect child STDERR into STDOUT at the runner boundary so non-fatal warnings no longer trigger false FAILED status while preserving return-code based failure detection.
Enforce shell-level STDERR-to-STDOUT redirection for the training entrypoint to satisfy stderr-strict job status checks.
